issues.description: Arbitrary font size values used: `text-[11px]`, `text-[1.55rem]`, `text-[1.8rem]`, `text-[2rem]`. These bypass the design system. The display heading (lines 123) uses responsive arbitrary sizes — if these are intentional brand typography values, they should be extracted as semantic typography tokens in globals.css (e.g., `text-hero-title`, `text-label-xs`).
issues.rule: No Arbitrary Values — use design system tokens
issues.suggestion: Define semantic type tokens in the shared `ui/globals.css` (e.g., `@theme { --text-label-xs: 0.6875rem; }`) and use `text-label-xs` instead of `text-[11px]`. For the display heading sizes, add a `text-display-*` scale.

issues.description: Arbitrary font size `text-[11px]` used for the cart quantity label. This is the same sub-xs size as in FeaturesSection — suggests a recurring need for a `text-label-xs` (11px) token that is missing from the design system.
issues.rule: No Arbitrary Values — use design system tokens
issues.suggestion: Add a `text-label-xs` semantic token (11px) to the shared theme and replace `text-[11px]` in both files.

issues.description: Arbitrary grid-column values: `grid-cols-[1fr_1fr_1fr_1fr_80px_32px]`. These fixed pixel columns for a data table layout have no design token equivalent.
issues.rule: No Arbitrary Values — use design system tokens
issues.suggestion: This is a borderline case for a table layout — consider extracting to a shared CSS class (e.g., `template-items-grid`) in a feature-level CSS module or utility class. However, if this is a one-off layout, keeping it inline is acceptable per KISS.

issues.description: Arbitrary grid-column values: `grid-cols-[1fr_1fr_100px_80px_100px]` used in both the header and data rows. The same value is repeated twice in the same file.
issues.suggestion: Extract to a shared CSS class or a local constant used with `cn()` so the column definition is DRY. E.g., `const COLS = 'grid-cols-[1fr_1fr_100px_80px_100px]'` used with `cn(COLS, ...)` — NOTE: per DRY rules, Tailwind class strings should become a component or utility class, not a JS constant. Consider a wrapping CSS class.

issues.description: Arbitrary grid-column values: `grid-cols-[160px_100px_1fr_90px_1fr_40px]` used in both the header and data rows. Same pattern as TemplateTable — grid-cols repeated twice.
issues.suggestion: Same as STYLE-004: extract the column definition to avoid duplication. A CSS utility class in `globals.css` or a scoped CSS module would be appropriate.

patterns.description: No raw Tailwind palette color classes found (text-red-*, bg-green-*, text-blue-*, etc.). All color usage correctly relies on semantic tokens (text-muted-foreground, text-destructive, bg-background, etc.).

All inline style={{ }} usages with color/background values are dynamic theme variables (e.g., theme.bg, theme.foreground, categoryColor) — these are correctly kept as dynamic styles since CSS variables cannot be used with Tailwind for truly dynamic runtime values.

The `text-[11px]` value appears in two separate apps (landing and store), suggesting a missing design token for a sub-xs label size. This is a recurring need that would benefit from a shared semantic token.
